exploration_log.aha_moment: The `chars_read` member is incremented on every byte read, so recording `chars_read - 4` right after reading the size gives a clean start baseline to compare against `start + document_size` after parsing.
exploration_log.hindsight: Nothing to change — the fix was straightforward once the byte-tracking mechanism was understood.

solve_notes: Both `parse_bson_internal()` and `parse_bson_array()` in `binary_reader.hpp` read a 4-byte document size prefix but never validate it. I captured `chars_read - 4` immediately after reading the size to get the start position, then after `parse_bson_element_list()` returns I compare `chars_read` against `start_position + document_size` and invoke `sax->parse_error` with `parse_error.110` when they differ.
task_text:
Fix binary_reader not validating BSON document size against consumed bytes

The BSON reader in `detail/input/binary_reader.hpp` reads the 4-byte
document size prefix but does not validate that the total bytes
consumed during parsing match the declared size. If the BSON data
contains extra trailing bytes within the declared document boundary,
they are silently ignored rather than flagged as corruption. Fix the
BSON parsing path in `binary_reader.hpp` to verify that the consumed
byte count matches the declared document size and throw
`parse_error.110` on mismatch.
